2604.11655.title: RPA-Check: A Multi-Stage Automated Framework for Evaluating Dynamic LLM-based Role-Playing Agents
2604.11655.paper_summary: The rapid adoption of Large Language Models (LLMs) in interactive systems has enabled the creation of dynamic, open-ended Role-Playing Agents (RPAs). However, evaluating these agents remains a significant challenge, as standard NLP metrics fail to capture the nuances of role adherence, logical consistency, and long-term narrative stability. This paper introduces RPA-Check, a multi-stage automated evaluation framework designed to objectively assess the performance of LLM-based RPAs in complex, constraints-heavy environments. Our methodology is based on a four-step pipeline: (1) Dimension Definition, establishing high-level qualitative behavioral criteria; (2) Augmentation, where these requirements are expanded into granular boolean checklist indicators; (3) Semantic Filtering, to ensure indicator objectivity, no redundancy and agent isolation; and (4) LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation, which employs chain-of-thought verification to score agent fidelity. We validate this framework by applying it to LLM Court, a serious game for forensic training involving several quantized local models. Experimental results across five distinct legal scenarios demonstrate the framework's ability to identify subtle trade-offs between model size, reasoning depth, and operational stability. Notably, the findings reveal an inverse relationship between parametric scale and procedural consistency, showing that smaller, adequately instruction-tuned models (8-9B) can outperform larger architectures prone to user-alignment bias or sycophancy. RPA-Check thus provides a standardized and reproducible metric for future research in generative agent evaluation within specialized domains.
2604.11655.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在互動系統中的快速採用使得動態、開放式角色扮演代理（RPAs）的創建成為可能。<lb/>然而，評估這些代理仍然是一個重大挑戰，因為標準的自然語言處理（NLP）指標無法捕捉角色遵循、邏輯一致性和長期敘事穩定性的細微差別。<lb/>本文介紹了RPA-Check，一個多階段自動評估框架，旨在客觀評估基於LLM的RPA在複雜且約束重重的環境中的表現。<lb/>我們的方法論基於四步驟流程： (1) 維度定義，建立高層次的定性行為標準； (2) 擴充，將這些要求擴展為細化的布林檢查指標； (3) 語義過濾，以確保指標的客觀性、無冗餘和代理隔離；以及 (4) LLM作為評判者的評估，利用思維鏈驗證來評分代理的忠實度。<lb/>我們通過將該框架應用於LLM Court，一個涉及幾個量化本地模型的法醫訓練嚴肅遊戲，來驗證這一框架。<lb/>在五種不同法律場景下的實驗結果顯示該框架能夠識別模型大小、推理深度和操作穩定性之間的微妙權衡。<lb/>值得注意的是，研究結果揭示了參數規模與程序一致性之間的反比關係，顯示較小、經過適當指導調整的模型（8-9B）可以超越較大架構，後者容易受到用戶對齊偏見或奉承的影響。<lb/>因此，RPA-Check為未來在專業領域內生成代理評估的研究提供了一個標準化和可重複的指標。
2604.11655.author: Riccardo Rosati et.al.
2604.11655.authors: Riccardo Rosati, Edoardo Colucci, Massimiliano Bolognini, Adriano Mancini, Paolo Sernani
